metadata.note: Baseline runs skipped — subagents hit rate limit. Inline execution only (with_skill). Comparison against without_skill not available this iteration.

expectations.evidence: Opens mid-scene with named character Marcus

expectations.text: Reel has 6+ scenes with time codes

expectations.evidence: Evidence-backed language, no motivational poster phrases
'Your first light roast might taste like blueberries. That's not a mistake.'

expectations.text: Reel references copper or cream palette
expectations.evidence: Copper (#B87333) and Cream (#F9F4EC) explicitly in aesthetic anchor

expectations.text: LinkedIn is thought-leadership, not Instagram copy
expectations.evidence: Analytical opening, subscription mention, different structure

expectations.text: Extracted business info from inline text
expectations.evidence: Cover note confirms correct extraction of all key fields
Blog and both social posts are on biophilic design and build longevity
Long considered sentences, no trend language, academic-adjacent framing

expectations.text: Carousel has 8 slides with green/sand palette
expectations.evidence: 8 slides; forest green and warm sand referenced in every visual direction
Different opening, analytical structure, performance data cited

notes: Baseline runs were not completed — subagents hit API rate limit. Comparison data unavailable for this iteration.
All 3 with-skill runs passed 100% of assertions (29/29 total assertions).
Eval 3 (verdant-architecture) had 9 assertions vs 10 for others — this is by design (one assertion is N/A for inline-only eval). Consider normalizing assertion count in future.
Word counts are all within range: 587 (apex), 564 (copper-ground), 561 (verdant). Skill instruction of 500-700 is being followed reliably.
Zero em dash violations across all 3 outputs — the craft rule is working.
Voice differentiation is the most subjective assertion and was passed by human judgment. Consider adding a more granular rubric in iteration 2.
Suggest running with real subagents after rate limit resets to get an independent baseline comparison.
